edges.description: Failure to mature COX2 prevents formation of a catalytically competent enzyme.
edges.source: COX18 Loss and Defective COX2 Maturation
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy failure in cardiomyocytes drives hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Bioenergetic deficit in peripheral nerve produces axonal sensory neuropathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Bioenergetic failure in nervous tissue contributes to neonatal encephalopathy in COX18-related disease.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Severe multisystem energy failure in neonatal COX18-related disease contributes to failure to thrive.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

nodes.description: Biallelic COX18 variants impair maturation and membrane insertion of the mtDNA-encoded COX2 subunit, preventing assembly of a functional Complex IV holoenzyme.
nodes.id: COX18 Loss and Defective COX2 Maturation

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: A biallelic variant in COX18 cause isolated Complex IV deficiency associated with neonatal encephalo-cardio-myopathy and axonal sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: A biallelic variant in COX18 cause isolated Complex IV deficiency associated with neonatal encephalo-cardio-myopathy and axonal sensory neuropathy.

nodes.description: Encephalopathy as part of the neonatal encephalo-cardio-myopathy.

evidence.reference_title: A biallelic variant in COX18 cause isolated Complex IV deficiency associated with neonatal encephalo-cardio-myopathy and axonal sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: A biallelic variant in COX18 cause isolated Complex IV deficiency associated with neonatal encephalo-cardio-myopathy and axonal sensory neuropathy.

evidence.reference_title: A biallelic variant in COX18 cause isolated Complex IV deficiency associated with neonatal encephalo-cardio-myopathy and axonal sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: The proband is a 19-months old female displaying hypertrophic cardiomyopathy at birth and myopathy with axonal sensory neuropathy and failure to thrive

evidence.reference_title: A biallelic variant in COX18 cause isolated Complex IV deficiency associated with neonatal encephalo-cardio-myopathy and axonal sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: The proband is a 19-months old female displaying hypertrophic cardiomyopathy at birth and myopathy with axonal sensory neuropathy and failure to thrive

nodes.description: Loss of functional COX blocks electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis, with prominent cardiac, muscle, and peripheral nerve involvement.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: Mammalian cytochrome c oxidase (COX) catalyses the transfer of reducing equivalents from cytochrome c to molecular oxygen and pumps protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane.

evidence.reference_title: A biallelic variant in COX18 cause isolated Complex IV deficiency associated with neonatal encephalo-cardio-myopathy and axonal sensory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: The proband is a 19-months old female displaying hypertrophic cardiomyopathy at birth and myopathy with axonal sensory neuropathy and failure to thrive
